input.prompt:
I'm migrating my MSTest projects from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.
My repo has a single Directory.Build.props at the root that is imported by
both production projects (e.g. `MyApp.csproj`) and test projects (e.g.
`MyApp.Tests.csproj`). I want to set `<OutputType>Exe</OutputType>` and the
MTP runner properties centrally, but I obviously don't want to turn my
library projects into executables. How do I write the condition in
Directory.Build.props so it only applies to the test projects?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm migrating my MSTest projects from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.
My repo has a single Directory.Build.props at the root that is imported by
both production projects (e.g. `MyApp.csproj`) and test projects (e.g.
`MyApp.Tests.csproj`). I want to set `<OutputType>Exe</OutputType>` and the
MTP runner properties centrally, but I obviously don't want to turn my
library projects into executables. How do I write the condition in
Directory.Build.props so it only applies to the test projects?

data.content:
Use **`Directory.Build.targets`** (not `.props`) and condition on `IsTestProject`, which is set by `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk`:

```xml
<!-- Directory.Build.targets -->
<Project>
  <PropertyGroup Condition="'$(IsTestProject)' == 'true'">
    <OutputType>Exe</OutputType>
    <EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
    <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
  </PropertyGroup>
</Project>
```

**Why `.targets` and not `.props`?** `Directory.Build.props` is imported *before* NuGet package props run, so `IsTestProject` (set by `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk`) isn't available yet. `.targets` is imported *after*, so the property is reliably set.

This means any project referencing `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` automatically gets the MTP settings; library projects are unaffected.
data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about how to conditionally set MSBuild properties in a `Directory.Build.props` file so that they only apply to test projects (not production/library projects) when migrating from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform (MTP).

This is a knowledge question — no need to explore a codebase. I can answer directly.

The standard approach is to use an MSBuild condition that checks for the presence of the `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` package reference or checks the project file name convention. The most robust approach is to use the `IsTestProject` property or condition on `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk`.


I'm weighing the tradeoffs between using the `IsTestProject` property that `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` sets versus relying on a naming convention like checking if the project name ends with `.Tests`. The timing is tricky since `IsTestProject` might not be available yet in `Directory.Build.props` depending on when package references are resolved, so a naming convention might be more reliable in practice.

The cleanest solution is to use `Directory.Build.targets` instead of `.props` because targets are imported after NuGet package props, meaning `IsTestProject` will definitely be set by then. I can condition on that property to apply test-specific settings only to projects that have the test SDK installed.
